text: Workers' Party performed strongly in GE2020, winning Sengkang GRC and retaining Aljunied GRC.
PAP maintained its supermajority in parliament with over 83% of seats. A strong mandate for governance.
PSP secured some votes in the West Coast GRC but fell short of winning a seat. Progress Singapore Party is still building momentum.
Disappointed with the lack of opposition representation in many constituencies. We need stronger checks and balances.
Pritam Singh's recognition as Leader of the Opposition is a watershed moment for Singapore's parliamentary democracy.
The PAP's handling of the Covid-19 pandemic has been efficient, but civil liberties concerns remain under the heightened restrictions.
SDP's alternative budget proposals make a lot of sense for lower-income households. More parties should put forward detailed policy platforms.
Lawrence Wong's leadership style is pragmatic and measured. He communicates policy clearly to the public.
The transport fee hike on top of rising cost of living is deeply frustrating. The government seems out of touch.
Workers' Party's town council management in Aljunied has improved substantially. Accountability matters.
GE2025 is approaching. Expecting fierce competition in marginal constituencies. Voters are more politically aware than ever.
PPP has yet to present a credible platform that resonates with young Singaporean voters.
